title: acetosa alpestris subsp. carpatica
intro: Acetosa alpestris subsp. carpatica, also known as the Alpine sorrel or Mountain sorrel, is a sub-species of the Acetosa alpestris plant, which belongs to the sorrel family (Rumex). It is a perennial herb that has been used for centuries in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of Acetosa alpestris subsp. carpatica include its ability to treat digestive issues, reduce fever, and act as an antiseptic for wounds, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and cholesterol levels. Herbal preparations of Acetosa alpestris subsp. carpatica typically involve making infusions or decoctions from the leaves, which can be consumed as a tea, or used topically to treat skin conditions.
